Safety Mar 25, 2026

Pilot Shares What It’s Like To Perform A Go-Around During Landing

A pilot reveals the reality of go-arounds—why they happen, how they’re flown, and why abandoning a landing is often the safest decision.

Go-arounds are standard risk-management procedures, not failures, with pilots trained to prioritize safety over schedule pressure or landing completion.

See Simple Flying →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Textron Aviation To Showcase Full Range Of Aircraft At Sun ’n Fun

Textron Aviation will display Cessna, Beechcraft, and Pipistrel aircraft together for the first time at Sun ’n Fun 2026.

Unified aircraft display signals Textron's intent to position its diverse brands as complementary solutions rather than competitors within the general aviation market.

See AVweb →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Cirrus posts strong 2025 with 691 SR Series deliveries, 106 Vision Jets

Cirrus Aircraft reported it delivered 691 SR Series piston aircraft and a record 106 Vision Jets in 2025,…

Vision Jet's 57% year-over-year growth signals accelerating single-engine jet adoption among owner-pilots despite premium pricing, reshaping general aviation's performance tier.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Webb Captures Saturn in Infrared

Saturn’s icy rings glow in this infrared view from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope released on March 25, 2026. Combining this image with another that NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope captured – also released on the same day – provides scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of the gas...

Webb's infrared imaging of Saturn reveals thermal properties invisible to optical telescopes, advancing planetary science methods applicable to exoplanet characterization.

See NASA →

PAI
Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

SMS Declaration of Compliance demonstrates operator accountability for safety management maturity and signals regulatory shift toward data-driven oversight rather than prescriptive rules.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Study Unveils Redevelopment Concepts For Burke Lakefront Airport

A new economic study outlines two potential mixed-use redevelopment plans for Burke Lakefront Airport, part of Mayor Justin Bibb’s effort to close the city-operated airport.

Municipal airport closures are increasingly justified through mixed-use redevelopment studies, signaling a shift in how cities balance aviation infrastructure with urban real estate value.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Spirit Airlines vs. Frontier Airlines: Comparing The 2 Ultra-Low-Cost Carriers' Seat Pitch And Carry-On Bag Restrictions

The two airlines offer a similar experience, but have slight variations.

Both carriers' increasingly restrictive seat pitch and baggage policies reflect intensifying competition to reduce costs, signaling that base fares alone may no longer sustain ultra-low-cost viability.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Webb, Hubble Share Most Comprehensive View of Saturn to Date

NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope and Hubble Space Telescope have teamed up to capture new views of Saturn, revealing the planet in strikingly different ways. Observing in complementary wavelengths of light, the two space observatories provide scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of...

Multi-wavelength observations from Webb and Hubble demonstrate how complementary sensor technologies reveal atmospheric details critical for understanding planetary dynamics and improving remote sensing methodologies.

See NASA →

PAI
Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

How to Assess Safety Risk

A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.

Part 5 compliance requires systematic risk assessment methodologies that extend beyond traditional hazard identification to quantify and mitigate safety exposure.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Why Did Antonov Construct The An-225 With 6 Engines?

A closer look at one of the coolest quirks of a legendary aircraft.

The An-225's six-engine configuration enabled unmatched payload capacity of 250+ tons by distributing thrust across multiple smaller turbofans rather than relying on fewer massive engines, optimizing both efficiency and structural feasibility for ultra-heavy cargo operations.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

10 Boeing Jets With The Longest Range Flying Today

Boeing’s longest-range jets reach over 8,500 NM, enabling ultra-long-haul flights.

Ultra-long-range Boeing aircraft exceed 8,500 nautical miles, enabling direct routes that reduce fuel costs and operational complexity for carriers on transcontinental and transpacific networks.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA-JAXA’s XRISM Telescope Clocks Hot Wind of Galaxy M82

For the first time, astronomers have directly measured the speed of superheated gas billowing from a cauldron of stellar activity at the heart of M82.

X-ray spectroscopy advances from space telescopes enable precise measurement of galactic phenomena, informing computational models applicable to aerospace propulsion and thermal dynamics research.

See NASA →

▶
General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Frederick Municipal Airport, MD (FDK)

Frederick Municipal is a small general and corporate aviation airport located just east of the city of Frederick, Maryland. The traffic mix runs the spectrum from single engine training aircraft up...

Frederick Municipal's diverse traffic mix from trainers to corporate jets positions regional airports as critical infrastructure for distributed aviation networks beyond major hubs.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

FAA Clears Higher Takeoff Weights For Boeing 787-9, -10

The FAA has approved higher takeoff weights for the Boeing 787-9 and 787-10, giving operators more flexibility to carry additional payload or extend range on long-haul routes.

Weight increases enable operators to optimize fuel-payload tradeoffs on long-haul routes, potentially improving unit economics on thin-margin international services.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Delta Suspends Flights On Major Long-Haul Route (Full Schedule Inside)

Delta's latest development follows other airlines in the market. See the latest information here!

Route suspensions signal airline capacity rebalancing amid demand fluctuations and competitive pressures on premium long-haul markets.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Press Release: ZIPAIR selects Axinom Stream for next-gen streaming

ZIPAIR Tokyo Inc. (“ZIPAIR”) and Axinom today announced the airline’s selection of Axinom Stream to power its modern onboard streaming platform as part of its in-flight entertainment (IFE) offering. Built on Axinom Stream, the solution enables DRM-protected Hollywood content delivery to passengers’...

ZIPAIR's adoption of Axinom Stream demonstrates how budget carriers are investing in premium streaming infrastructure to differentiate passenger experience and compete with full-service competitors.

See Runway Girl Network →

▶
Safety Mar 25, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit design at LAX creates a specific geometry that increases pilot confusion during high-traffic operations, requiring enhanced crew resource management during ground operations.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Awards Astrophysics Postdoctoral Fellowships for 2026

The highly competitive NASA Hubble Fellowship Program (NHFP) recently named 24 new fellows to its 2026 class. The NHFP enables outstanding postdoctoral scientists to pursue independent research in any area of NASA Astrophysics, using theory, observations, simulations, experimentation, or instrument...

NASA's competitive fellowship program develops the scientific talent pipeline that drives innovations applicable to aerospace technology and satellite systems.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA-ISRO Satellite Captures Pacific Northwest Through Clouds

Seattle and Portland, Oregon, are among the cloudiest cities in the United States. But that infamous cloud cover is no match for the U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar), which is designed to peer straight through clouds. Doing so allows scientists to study the...

NISAR's cloud-penetrating radar capability enables continuous Earth observation over persistently cloudy regions, reducing weather-dependent gaps in critical infrastructure and environmental monitoring data.

See NASA →

Military Mar 25, 2026

New Images Show B-2 Spirit Bombers Departing for Iran Mission with Unknown White Patches on Wings

Recently released photos show B-2 stealth bombers preparing to launch for a mission over Iran last week, with multiple unidentified white patches added along the leading edges of their wings. The U.S. Central Command has released new photos of the B-2A Spirit stealth bomber, one of the assets that,...

The white patches on B-2 wings likely represent field-applied countermeasures or sensor upgrades, suggesting ongoing operational modifications to stealth aircraft for specific threat environments.

See The Aviationist →

Industry Mar 25, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operators can simultaneously expand airport capacity and reduce environmental impact by implementing integrated optimization across airline scheduling, ground operations, and airspace management systems.

See MDPI →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

Digital Airman Certificate Bill Clears U.S. House

Measure would permit pilots to present electronic credentials during FAA inspections.

Digital airman certificates could streamline compliance checks and reduce administrative burden for pilots, while enabling the FAA to modernize credential verification systems.

See AVweb →

Military Mar 25, 2026

Brazil unveils first Gripen E fighter assembled on home soil

Brazil has unveiled the first Gripen E fighter assembled on its own soil, a milestone for the Saab-Embraer…

Brazil's domestic Gripen E assembly demonstrates successful technology transfer and positions Embraer as a strategic aerospace manufacturer capable of supporting advanced fighter production in Latin America.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

China Eastern orders over 100 Airbus aircraft, plans to retire at least 53 A320s

China Eastern Airlines has placed a $15.802 billion Airbus order for 101 A320neo family aircraft, comprised of A320neo,…

China Eastern's massive A320neo order signals confidence in regional growth despite fleet modernization costs tied to retiring older narrowbodies.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Mar 25, 2026

UK, Turkey sign Eurofighter Typhoon support deal to advance fighter export

The UK and Turkey have signed a new support and training agreement tied to Ankara’s Eurofighter Typhoon purchase,…

Strategic support agreements beyond initial aircraft sales are becoming critical differentiators for fighter jet exports in competitive markets.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Wizz Air CCO on recovering the cost lead, offering Wi-Fi, and hedging fuel

On March 25, 2026, Wizz Air announced that it had signed a deal with Barcelona-based inflight technology provider…

Ultra-low-cost carriers are now monetizing ancillary services like Wi-Fi to recover margin compression from fuel hedging strategies, signaling a shift toward hybrid revenue models.

See AeroTime Hub →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

House Moves to Bring Back Civil Overland Supersonic Flight

Measure would direct FAA to write new regulations for civil aircraft flying faster than Mach 1.

Regulatory reform could enable a new market segment, but operators must overcome sonic boom mitigation challenges that previously blocked overland supersonic service.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Make-Or-Break Week: United Airlines Enters Final Contract Talks With Flight Attendants

Representatives of the carrier's cabin crew are hoping to be able to lock down a tentative agreement.

United's flight attendant negotiations enter critical phase as labor costs remain a pivotal factor in post-pandemic airline profitability recovery.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

DJI just nuked drone prices across the board

Something strange is happening to DJI drone prices right now. And if you’ve ever even briefly considered buying a drone, this is one of those moments where ignoring it might come back to haunt you. Because this isn’t a typical seasonal sale or a small round of discounts tucked away on a product...

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy may signal intensifying competition in the commercial drone market and could reshape customer acquisition patterns for the entire industry.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Air Arabia debuts Panasonic Avionics multi-orbit IFC

Already a customer of Panasonic Avionics’ eXW wireless inflight entertainment system, UAE-based Air Arabia is now flying with the IFEC giant’s Ku-band multi-orbit onboard connectivity solution, Runway Girl Network can reveal. The all-Airbus A320 Family operator is among a cluster of carriers...

Air Arabia's adoption of Panasonic's multi-orbit Ku-band connectivity demonstrates growing airline confidence in hybrid satellite solutions that reduce single-orbit latency dependencies.

See Runway Girl Network →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

FAA Letter Says Whiteman Airport Obligated to Stay Open

Los Angeles County still continues to study closure.

Despite ongoing closure studies, the FAA has legally bound Whiteman Airport to remain operational, potentially blocking local efforts to repurpose the facility.

See AVweb →

International Mar 25, 2026

Philippine carriers cut flights, secure fuel as government declares emergency

The Philippines has become the first country to declare a national state of energy emergency amid escalating Middle…

Airlines facing fuel supply disruptions must implement aggressive capacity reductions, signaling that geopolitical energy crises now directly constrain regional aviation operations and require government intervention.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Southwest Airlines Cabin Reconfiguration Increases Seat Pitch, Reducing Overall Passenger Capacity

Southwest Airlines has added extra-legroom seats to its fleet.

Southwest's capacity reduction through increased seat pitch represents a strategic bet that premium seating revenue and improved passenger satisfaction will offset lower load factors.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

$15.8 Billion Vote Of Confidence: Airbus Scores Massive 101-Plane Deal

The European planemaker continues to make big steps forward in a key market.

Airbus's 101-plane order signals sustained demand recovery and validates its competitive positioning against Boeing in a critical market segment.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Why Can’t The Airbus A350 Be Powered By Any Other Engine Type?

The Airbus A350 and Rolls-Royce Trent XWB are a match made in aviation heaven.

The A350's airframe design is aerodynamically optimized exclusively for the Trent XWB's bypass ratio and thrust characteristics, making engine substitution technically and economically infeasible.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

JetBlue Flight Halted After Bizarre Runway Encounter With Coyote

The aircraft did not suffer any damage and was back in the air shortly after the incident, transporting passengers to their original destination.

Wildlife intrusions at major airports remain a persistent operational risk requiring enhanced perimeter security protocols beyond current deterrent systems.

See Simple Flying →
